items.headline: Rangers' Nimmo leaves game against Diamondbacks with sprained left ankle
items.description:
ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Texas Rangers right fielder Brandon Nimmo left Tuesday night's game against the Arizona Diamondbacks in the sixth inning with a sprained left ankle.
Nimmo's left foot hit the edge of first baseman Ildemaro Vargas' foot as he tried to leg out an infield single and he came up limping. He stayed in the game for a few pitches before he was replaced by pinch-runner Sam Haggerty.

items.headline: Phillies' Kyle Schwarber says 'I can get better' as home run streak hits 5 straight games
items.description:
BOSTON (AP) — Kyle Schwarber feels his surge is just getting started.
Scwharber tied a Phillies record by homering for the fifth consecutive game, hitting his major league-high 17th to lead Philadelphia over the Boston Red Sox 2-1 on Tuesday night.

items.headline: Cal Raleigh tried a full-uniform shower. The next night, his bat finally woke up
items.description:
HOUSTON (AP) — Cal Raleigh was desperate to shake things up while mired in the longest hitless streak in the majors this season.
So on the advice of pitcher Logan Gilbert, Seattle's star catcher showered in full uniform after Monday night's game.

items.headline: Angel Martinez homers, Guardians strike out 13 and top Angels
items.description:
CLEVELAND (AP) — Angel Martinez homered and Cleveland’s pitchers struck out 13 as the Guardians kept up their home mastery of the Los Angeles Angels with a 3-2 victory on Tuesday night.
Martinez, Patrick Bailey and Bryan Rocchio drove in runs for Cleveland, which improved to 29-4 against the Angels at Progressive Field since 2015. The Guardians have won the first two games in the series despite being outhit twice.

items.headline: Dodgers give Shohei Ohtani a DH break as his offensive slump deepens
items.description:
LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani is getting a break from the batter's box for one day and possibly longer.
The struggling Los Angeles Dodgers superstar is 4 for 36 at the plate this month, with just six home runs on the season and none in May. He's batting .233 with 16 RBIs.

items.headline: Mets outfield prospect A.J. Ewing delivers in big league debut after fast rise through minors
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — True to his baseball tools, speedy outfielder A.J. Ewing made a rapid rise through the minors.
He's certainly off to a fast start with the New York Mets, too.

items.headline: Pirates place Joey Bart on the 10-day IL with a left foot infection and call up Endy Rodriguez
items.description:
PITTSBURGH (AP) — The Pittsburgh Pirates placed catcher Joey Bart on the 10-day injured list on Tuesday with a left foot infection.
Bart is hitting .259 with two homers and nine RBIs for the Pirates while splitting starting catching duties with Henry Davis.

items.headline: Braves place Sean Murphy on IL with fractured finger; sign Sandy León
items.description:
ATLANTA (AP) — The Braves placed catcher Sean Murphy on the injured list with a fractured left middle finger Tuesday, and manager Walt Weiss said he could be out up to eight weeks.
The Braves signed free agent catcher Sandy León to replace Murphy, who missed the first 35 games of the season rehabbing from right hip surgery in September. The 37-year-old León had been catching in an independent Mexican League. León played five games for the Braves last season, his only MLB action since 2023.

items.headline: Orioles lefty reliever Wolfram placed on IL; Wells recalled
items.description:
BALTIMORE (AP) — The Baltimore Orioles placed reliever Grant Wolfram on the 15-day injured list Tuesday with a lower back strain and recalled pitcher Tyler Wells from Triple-A Norfolk.
Wolfram is 1-1 with a 4.85 ERA in 16 games. The 6-foot-7, 250-pound lefty has 20 strikeouts and just one walk in 13 innings.

items.headline: Athletics shortstop Jacob Wilson goes on 10-day injured list with dislocated left shoulder
items.description:
WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Athletics shortstop Jacob Wilson was placed on the 10-day injured list Tuesday because of a dislocated left shoulder.
Wilson was hurt during the fifth inning of a 2-1 loss Sunday at Baltimore after diving for Gunnar Henderson's infield single. His IL placement was retroactive to Monday.
